name: postgresql-common
title: postgresql-common
description:
PostgreSQL database-cluster manager
The postgresql-common package provides a structure under which
multiple versions of PostgreSQL may be installed and/or multiple
clusters maintained at one time.
.
The commands provided are pg_conftool, pg_createcluster, pg_ctlcluster,
pg_dropcluster, pg_lsclusters, pg_renamecluster, pg_upgradecluster,
pg_virtualenv.
.
PostgreSQL is a fully featured object-relational database management
system. It supports a large part of the SQL standard and is designed
to be extensible by users in many aspects. Its features include ACID
transactions, foreign keys, views, sequences, subqueries, triggers,
outer joins, multiversion concurrency control, and user-defined types
and functions.

commands.name: pg_config
commands.description: pg_config dostarcza informacji na temat zainstalowanej wersji PostgreSQL.

commands.name: pg_conftool
commands.description: read and edit PostgreSQL cluster configuration files

commands.name: pg_lsclusters
commands.description: show information about all PostgreSQL clusters

commands.name: pg_upgradecluster
commands.description: upgrade an existing PostgreSQL cluster to a new major version.

commands.name: pg_virtualenv
commands.description: Create a throw-away PostgreSQL environment for running regression tests

commands.name: pg_updatedicts
commands.description: build PostgreSQL dictionaries from myspell/hunspell ones

commands.name: pg_archivecleanup
commands.description: clean up PostgreSQL WAL archive files

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use pg_backupcluster because PostgreSQL database-cluster manager.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use pg_backupcluster as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `pg_backupcluster [options] version cluster action` as documented upstream for pg_backupcluster.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair pg_backupcluster with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `pg_backupcluster --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: pg_backupcluster - simple pg_basebackup and pg_dump front-end.

Install with: sudo apt install postgresql-common
